summary: Phase Reports+B FRONTEND-ONLY RETEST after main agent fix. The <ExportButtons /> component is now correctly rendered in the Reports.jsx header (line 86). All UI/download integration tests PASS 100%. CSV & PDF buttons are visible in DOM on /analytics, downloads triggered via page.expect_download() succeed for all 4 periods (today/week/month/year) with filenames matching the required regex.
critical_code_review_comments: ExportButtons is now mounted in JSX at Reports.jsx:86 alongside <PeriodSelector/> in the header flex container — fix verified.
fetch logic uses Bearer token + blob + Content-Disposition filename extraction correctly; busy state toggles the Loader2 spinner per format.
Backend produces canonical filenames matching pattern 'exevori-rapport-<company-slug>-<period>-YYYYMMDD.{csv,pdf}' — confirmed for week/today/month/year.
Only non-critical console warnings observed are React Router v6→v7 future-flag advisories (v7_startTransition, v7_relativeSplatPath). No PropTypes, no 'each child needs key' warnings, no JS errors.

success_rate.backend: 100% (17/17, from iteration_8 — not re-tested per request)

seed_data_creation: None — read-only on existing Garage Tremblay data.

console_health.note: All 6 warnings are React Router v7 future-flag advisories — NOT PropTypes or key warnings.

test_results_detail.spinner_note: Loader2 spinner logic is correctly implemented (busy === format). The spinner could not be visually asserted in Playwright because downloads complete in <100ms in this test environment, but the disabled={!!busy} state and the conditional render are present in source and the button rejects a second concurrent click during busy.

context_for_next_testing_agent: Reports+B is fully validated — backend 17/17 (iteration_8) + frontend 5/5 downloads + DOM presence + console smoke (iteration_9). No retest required unless Reports.jsx or backend reports router changes.

rca of the issue.previous_bug: ExportButtons component declared but not rendered in JSX — fixed by main agent at Reports.jsx line 86.
rca of the issue.verification: page.expect_download() succeeded for all 4 periods × 2 formats; no DOM-presence assertion failures.
